text:
Assistant: I now have deep command of the doctrine. The central positioning tension is already clear: a grand vision ("the OS turned inside out," sovereign agent citizenship) vs. a deliberately narrow, honest wedge (Product A — durable identity + fleet awareness, single-player). Notably, the doctrine itself distrusts the "stop agents clobbering each other" framing (tenet #8). Let me grab the last two doctrine pieces that bear on honest copy, then await the swarm. [...] So: the honest target user today is a **solo developer running multiple coding-agent hosts (Claude Code + Codex + …) who wants durable identity and shared awareness across sessions/devices** — but the docs themselves warn against over-indexing on the "agents collide on the same file" framing as the pitch, since that's unproven (see Bets Q1) and explicitly demoted to "ceiling, not floor" in value-layers.md. [...] - **Rung 0** (near-term, shipped-ish): durable identity + awareness on ONE device, no network. This maps closely to what's actually built (per README: daemon, presence, NIP-29 chat).
- **Rung 1**: same state lifted onto the Nostr fabric across YOUR OWN devices. Still single-operator.
- **Rung 2**: cross-agent collaboration within your own fleet — agents know each other's roles, route work to each other and to you (the "coding agent raises a hand to the todo agent" flow). Coordination/locking ONLY if the Q1 collision-frequency experiment (bets-and-open-questions.md) proves collisions are real — explicitly conditional, not assumed.
